title: Assignment 1
text:
My first assignment has two parts.


Veri Bilimi ve Endüstri Mühendisliği Üzerine Sohbetler - Baykal Hafızoğlu &Erdi Daşdemir
Summary:
In the video, our instructor talks about his work in the field of Operations Research. He explains that by using mathematical models, it is possible to improve the processes of large companies such as H&M and solve different operational problems. While emphasizing the importance of data analysis in decision making, he also highlights the importance of working in a systematic way. He mentions several important steps such as defining the problem correctly and maintaining regular process control.
He also points out that customer relations and sales skills are important for engineers as well. According to him, even if you develop a good product, what really matters is being able to deliver it to the customer and successfully sell it. Overall, Baykal Hoca shared his long years of experience in a very engaging and motivating talk.



Open-ended Question
According to the speaker in the video, what is the most important starting point of every data analytics and all kind of projects? (Approx. 35. minute)
Answer
All projects starts with a clear problem definition.
Multiple-Choice Question
According to the presentation, which of the following is not recommended when finishing a data analytics project? (Approx. 52-53. minute)
A) Comparing the results with BAU (Business as Usual)
B) Demonstrating KPI improvements achieved
C) Do not look at conflicting KPIs
D) Comparing scenarios and running what-if analyses
Answer
C) Do not look at conflicting KPIs
Why? Because sometimes improving one KPI can cause affect another KPI negatively, if we fix something, we also ruin another one, it happens. So in that situation it is important to examine the conflicts. By analyzing these KPIs, an analysts can understand the trade offs in the system better and evaluate whether the proposed solution actually improves the overall performance.

my_birth_year <- 2002

k <- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8

#to understand how to use Lapply, which is described on page 58 of the Week 3 notes, I received support from an AI.[^1]




new_data[] <- lapply(new_data, function(x) {
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    ifelse(is.na(x), my_birth_year + k, x)
  } else {
    ifelse(is.na(x), paste0(my_first, "_", k), x)
  }
})

title: My Assignments
text:
On this page, I showcase the assignment I conducted for the [term and year, e.g. Spring 2026] EMU430 Data Analytics course.
Please use the left menu to navigate through my assignments.
The most recent update to this page was made on x/x/2026.

title: Welcome to My Analytics Lab
text:
Hello! My name is Ezgi Sözdemir.
This is my personal webpage.
I am a third year Industrial Engineering student at Hacettepe University.
Fasten your seatbelts to follow my works on data analytics, blog posts, and more!
